California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April 20, 1989 
Lynne Andersen 
Legislative Assistant 
Assernblyrnember Filante's Office 
state Capitol, Room 5135 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-234 
Dear Ms. Andersen: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on April 18, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact John McLean 
an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours, 
~~[.~ 
~a~hryn~~. Donovan 
Acting General Counsel

SPAGNOLI DE AT Al AT AMINI ) 
Subject: regcard 
MEMORANDUM 
TO: 
John McLean 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
FROM: 
Lynne Andersen, Legislative Assistant 
DATE: 
April 17, 1989 
RE: 
9th Assembly District Small Business Conference 
Enclosed is a pamphlet, to be produced at state expense, for 
our 1989 Small Business Conference to be held in San Rafael 
on May 13, 1989. 
The pamphlet has been approved under the 
public meeting exemption by pat Foster in the Legislative 
Counsel's office. 
I would like approval by the FPPC, in writing, under the 
public meeting exemption. 
The pamphlet would be sent to 
approximately 12,000 small business owners in the 9th A.D. 
I 
would also like to clarify a few parts of the pamphlet: 
1) The pamphlet would be three-fold, mailed bulk rate 
in the member's envelopes. 
The front page indicates 
that the conference is sponsored by the member. 
If 
approved, can an Assembly and/or State seal be used? 
2) The agenda page lists three small business topics the 


member will address, in addition to question and 
answers on additional legislative matters. 
3) The "Open Letter" expands on some of those small 
business issues. 
4) The registration card lists the Marin County Chamber 
of Commerce as the return card recipient, as 
recommended by the Legislative Counsel's Office. 
5) The cost of the conference covers room rental and 
refreshments only. 
Thank you for your review. 
I would like an answer as soon as 
possible, as the conference date is rapidly approaching. 
Please contact me with any questions you may have at 5-7827. 


1989 SMALL BUSINESS CONFERENCE 
Sponsored by 
ASSEMBLYMAN BILL FILANTE, M.D.

In cooperation with

AN OPEN LETTER TO ALL 9TH A.D 
SMALL BUSINESSES: 
It is my pleasure to invite you to 
a conference designed for the small 
business owner. 
I am bringing together speakers 
and panelists on a variety of topics 
to help in development of your 
business, help in computer 
technology, and a discussion on the 
workers compensation and mandated 
health insurance debates currently 
raging in Sacramento. 
Here in the North Bay, small 
business accounts for much of our 
economic prosperity. 
However, these 
same small firms feel the worst 
pinch from excessive government 
regulation. 
Mandated health insurance for 
employers of five people or more is 
a real possibility this year. 
As 
chairman of a legislative task 
force, I 
am working on a solution

June 15, 1988 
Betty M. Brown 
Legal Documents Examiner 
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 
Dear Ms. Brown: 
Name: 
CLIFFORD L. ALLENBY 
Re: 
Statement of Economic Interests 
Type of Statement:· Assuming Office 
Date of Statement Due: 
March 3, 1988 
As per my telephone conversation with Linda Moureaux of your staff on 
June 14, 1988, the following is an explanation as to why Mr. Clifford L. 
Allenby's Form 730 Assuming Office statement was not filed in a timely 
manner: 
The Secretary of the Health and Welfare Agency (HWA) is, by statute 
(Section 4520-4523 of the Welfare & Institutions Code), a member of 
the State Council on Developmental Disabilities (SCDD). 
Mr. 
Allenby was appointed as Agency Secretary in 1986. 
Ms. Barbara 
Hooker, Assistant HWA Secretary, represents the HWA Secretary with 
voting privileges at all Council meetings. 
Prior to Mr. Allenby's 
assuming office, Ms. Hooker was the HWA Secretary's representative. 
On January 12, 1987, I requested Mr. Allenby file a Form 730 as a 
member of SCDD. 
Shortly thereafter, I recall receiving a phone 
call from a member of Mr. Allenby's staff stating that Mr. Allenby 
did not need to file as Ms. Barbara Hooker was his designated 
. representative. 
I then called FPPC requesting clarification and 
spoke to you on January 14, 1987. 
My notes indicate that you 
explained: 
1) Only representatives file; and 2) Alternates and 
Directors must both file. 
Based on this information, I did not 
pursue Mr. Allenby's Form 730 as Ms. Hooker was his designated 
representative. 
On January 29, 1988, I spoke with Lynn Martin of your staff, and it 
was explained to me that all members must file a Form 730, 
1507-21st STREET - ROOM 320 • SACRAMENTO, CA 95814. TELEPHONE (916) 322-8481 


Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
June 21, 1988 
Arlene A. Nishimura 
state Council on Developmental 
Disabilities 
1507 -
21st street, Room 320 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Nishimura: 
Re: 
88-234 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 21, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Jeanette Turvill, in the Legal 
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
• 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, we will contact you 
shortly to advise you as to the information needed. 
If your 
request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as 
quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. 
Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
CA~ h~ JJ~J.I-J..~ 
Diane M. Griffiths ~ 
General Counsel

AN OPEN LETTER TO ALL 9TH A.D 
SMALL BUSINESSES: 
It is my pleasure to invite you to 
a conference designed for the small 
business owner. 
I am bringing together speakers 
and panelists on a variety of topics 
to help in development of your 
business, help in computer 
technology, and a discussion on the 
workers compensation and mandated 
health insurance debates currently 
raging in Sacramento. 
Here in the North Bay, small 
business accounts for much of our 
economic prosperity. 
However, these 
same small firms feel the worst 
pinch from excessive government 
regulation. 
Mandated health insurance for 
employers of five people or more is 
a real possibility this year. 
As 
chairman of a legislative task 
force, I 
am working on a solution

Dear Ms. Nishimura: 
Re: 
88-234 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 21, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission April 20, 1989 Lynne Andersen Legislative Assistant Assernblyrnember Filante's Office state Capitol, Room 5135 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Letter No. 89-234 Dear Ms. Andersen: Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on April 18, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to information needed. If your request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. KED:plh Very truly yours, ~~[.~ ~a~hryn~~. Donovan Acting General Counsel I N T E R 0 F FIe E M E M 0 RAN DUM Date: From: Dept: Tel No: 17-Apr-1989 04:14pm PDT LYNNE ANDERSEN ANDERSEN LY AT Al AT AC09 TO: Debbie Spagnoli SPAGNOLI DE AT Al AT AMINI ) Subject: regcard MEMORANDUM TO: John McLean Fair Political Practices Commission FROM: Lynne Andersen, Legislative Assistant DATE: April 17, 1989 RE: 9th Assembly District Small Business Conference Enclosed is a pamphlet, to be produced at state expense, for our 1989 Small Business Conference to be held in San Rafael on May 13, 1989. The pamphlet has been approved under the public meeting exemption by pat Foster in the Legislative Counsel's office. I would like approval by the FPPC, in writing, under the public meeting exemption. The pamphlet would be sent to approximately 12,000 small business owners in the 9th A.D. I would also like to clarify a few parts of the pamphlet: 1) The pamphlet would be three-fold, mailed bulk rate in the member's envelopes. The front page indicates that the conference is sponsored by the member. If approved, can an Assembly and/or State seal be used? 2) The agenda page lists three small business topics the member will address, in addition to question and answers on additional legislative matters. 3) The "Open Letter" expands on some of those small business issues. 4) The registration card lists the Marin County Chamber of Commerce as the return card recipient, as recommended by the Legislative Counsel's Office. 5) The cost of the conference covers room rental and refreshments only. Thank you for your review. I would like an answer as soon as possible, as the conference date is rapidly approaching. Please contact me with any questions you may have at 5-7827. 1989 SMALL BUSINESS CONFERENCE Sponsored by ASSEMBLYMAN
